timeline.event: Corporate high-intensity role begins. 65-hour weeks set in immediately. Career climbs fast; stress and health begin their slow divergence.
Six months in. Promotion track looks promising. Savings growing steadily. Health decline is gradual enough to ignore — stress is background noise at this stage.
Egg freezing appointment booked for Month 12. Stress approaching a tipping point. Health has dropped 10 points since start. Savings are on track to cover the procedure.
Egg freezing completed. $12,000 withdrawn. Procedure stress spike pushes stress to 83. Fertility flexibility jumps by 50 points. Savings take a visible dip but remain positive.
BURNOUT #1 — Three consecutive months above stress threshold 70. Body forces a reset: health drops sharply, career momentum slips. Recovery period brings stress back to 45.
Recovery phase stabilising. Career nearly back to pre-burnout trajectory. Health has not recovered — the deficit is structural, not temporary. Savings fully rebuilt.
Career ceiling reached at 100. Stress climbing again. Health erosion continues. Second burnout is imminent — same pattern as before.
BURNOUT #2 — Career maxed, nowhere to channel the stress productively. Health drops to 37. Second forced recovery. The health floor is now dangerously low.
Mid-simulation checkpoint. Two years in: savings strong, fertility secured — but health is at 36, down from 80 at start. The structural cost of this path is undeniable.
Final stretch begins. Savings near $78K. Career at ceiling. Stress climbing toward third burnout threshold — pattern is now cyclical. Health at 30.
BURNOUT #3 — Health now at 18, in clinical risk zone. Career dips to 95 again. Savings continue compounding regardless, now over $83K. Financial plan succeeded; health plan failed.
Simulation complete. Career peaked at 100. Savings at $94K — well above target. Egg freezing secured fertility flexibility. However: health at 14/100, 3 burnouts sustained.

what_if_flags: Reducing work hours to 50/week eliminates the -1/month health penalty and delays first burnout by ~6 months
Raising health priority to 4 would apply a protective multiplier, reducing net health loss by ~20 points
Moving egg freezing to Month 6 avoids the compounding stress-on-stress effect at Month 12
